mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures consumer demand momentum; higher sales translate into expected revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, producing positive forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns following above-median RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, embedded monetary accommodation removes tail-risk from equity valuations; incremental easing generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as discount rates compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative sixty-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and a fall in the unemployment rate indicate a tight labor market where wage-driven consumption boosts corporate earnings; the joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the PAYEMS and UNRATE interaction term is below 0.04 over any two-year window, or the joint signal underperforms the individual PAYEMS signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment recoveries, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal capacity utilization normalization and inventory rebuilding, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this relationship weakens outside of slack labor markets.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes do not produce statistically significant positive sixty-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates more than 0.3% month-over-month, equities face margin compression fears and discount rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day returns are positive in more than fifty-five percent of instances when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts below six hundred thousand units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of observations when HOUST is below six hundred.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevation in the unemployment rate above 8% indicates deficient aggregate demand and weak household balance sheets, elevating equity risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving manufacturing profitability and operating leverage for industrial sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any two-year rolling window.
